nodes.label: AI as research starting point
nodes.grounding_span: I'll use AI for some search; some fact checking/research; document verification; ideation

nodes.label: AI as systematic troubleshooter

nodes.label: AI for efficient document comparison
nodes.label_negative: AI full-batch ordering without human guidance
nodes.grounding_span: We typically figure out the order first then do a combination of small batch then request a full-batch

nodes.label: AI for voice identification in documents
nodes.grounding_span: models have not had success with identifying voice in written form

nodes.label: AI for writing tasks (emails, captions, blogs)
nodes.grounding_span: I'll sometimes use AI for ideation if I am having a hard time coming up with a topic but I'll never use it to generate text for the blog

nodes.label: AI as collaborative conversational partner

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and verification
nodes.grounding_span: How do we verify the work of AI when much of it happens in a blackbox?

nodes.label: AI as tool for routine legal work
nodes.grounding_span: they all chose to ignore the hallucinated cases

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: Time is often the primary concern

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: we will never assume the results of AI and always verify by hand

nodes.label: Intellectual engagement and deeper understanding
nodes.grounding_span: editing your own writing can provide spontaneous understanding

nodes.label: Trustworthiness and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: Trust is important, and the best way to have trust is to verify

nodes.label: Satisfaction with targeted brainstorming

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination in legal research
